reasoning: The Trump administration explicitly rescinded the Biden-era "AI Diffusion Rule" in May 2025, which had established worldwide export controls on advanced semiconductors and AI model weights. Instead, BIS shifted to a "due diligence" model, issuing guidance that relies on existing Catch-All controls and General Prohibition 10 aimed primarily at "Country Group D:5" (e.g., China). Because the resolution criteria require that any new controls "must apply broadly, and must not be targeted or limited to a single country or entity," these new D:5-targeted enforcement rules are highly unlikely to qualify as a YES. Given that the administration just executed a major policy pivot away from broad/global AI export controls in May, the probability of them introducing new expanded global rules before the end of 2025 is quite low. The current market prediction of 0.52 likely overestimates the chances of a new qualifying broad rule, missing the finality of the May shift toward deregulation and targeted screening over blanket controls.
